query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
(750014, 'Prophetic literature in the Bible includes which genres?', 'multiple_choice', '["Only poetry","Oracles, visions, symbolic actions, and apocalyptic","Only narrative","Only law"]', 'Oracles, visions, symbolic actions, and apocalyptic', 1),
(750014, 'The Hebrew term "rîb" refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["A blessing","A covenant lawsuit speech","A love song","A creation account"]', 'A covenant lawsuit speech', 2),
(750014, 'Symbolic language in prophecy should be interpreted:', 'multiple_choice', '["Always literally","Always allegorically","According to context and biblical usage","According to personal preference"]', 'According to context and biblical usage', 3),
(750014, 'Conditional prophecies are characterized by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Absolute certainty regardless of response","Outcomes dependent on human response","Only positive outcomes","Only negative outcomes"]', 'Outcomes dependent on human response', 4),
(750014, 'Jeremiah 18:7-10 teaches that God may:', 'multiple_choice', '["Never change His plans","Relent from announced judgment if people repent","Always fulfill prophecy literally","Ignore human response"]', 'Relent from announced judgment if people repent', 5),
(750014, 'Apocalyptic literature is characterized by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Simple, literal language","Symbolic imagery, cosmic conflict, and divine intervention","Historical narrative only","Legal codes"]', 'Symbolic imagery, cosmic conflict, and divine intervention', 6),
(750014, 'The grammatical-historical method of interpretation focuses on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Allegorical meanings","The original meaning in historical context","Modern applications only","Personal feelings"]', 'The original meaning in historical context', 7),
(750014, 'Numbers in prophetic literature often have:', 'multiple_choice', '["No significance","Symbolic meaning","Only literal meaning","Random assignment"]', 'Symbolic meaning', 8),
(750014, 'Progressive revelation means that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Later revelation contradicts earlier","God reveals His plan gradually through history","All truth was revealed at once","Prophecy is unreliable"]', 'God reveals His plan gradually through history', 9),
(750014, 'The interpreter of prophecy must consider:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only the future application","Historical context, literary form, and canonical connections","Only personal experience","Only church tradition"]', 'Historical context, literary form, and canonical connections', 10),
(750014, 'Explain the importance of understanding literary genre when interpreting prophetic literature.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Understanding genre helps interpreters recognize the conventions and expectations of different types of prophetic literature, preventing misinterpretation of symbolic language as literal or vice versa.', 11),
(750015, 'The Protoevangelium in Genesis 3:15 promises:', 'multiple_choice', '["A political deliverer","The seed of the woman crushing the serpent","Immediate restoration","An angelic savior"]', 'The seed of the woman crushing the serpent', 1),
(750015, 'The Davidic covenant promised:', 'multiple_choice', '["A temporary kingdom","An eternal throne and kingdom","Wealth for David''s descendants","Military conquest only"]', 'An eternal throne and kingdom', 2),
(750015, 'Isaiah 53 describes the Messiah as:', 'multiple_choice', '["A conquering king only","A suffering servant","A military leader","A wealthy ruler"]', 'A suffering servant', 3),
(750015, 'Typology in prophetic fulfillment refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random connections","OT persons, events, or institutions that foreshadow Christ","Allegorical interpretation","Literal prediction only"]', 'OT persons, events, or institutions that foreshadow Christ', 4),
(750015, 'Micah 5:2 prophesied the Messiah''s birthplace as:', 'multiple_choice', '["Jerusalem","Nazareth","Bethlehem","Egypt"]', 'Bethlehem', 5),
(750015, 'The Servant Songs in Isaiah are found in chapters:', 'multiple_choice', '["1-5","40-55","60-66","All of Isaiah"]', '40-55', 6),
(750015, 'Psalm 22 prophetically describes:', 'multiple_choice', '["David''s coronation","The crucifixion of Christ","The Exodus","The flood"]', 'The crucifixion of Christ', 7),
(750015, 'Matthew''s Gospel emphasizes fulfillment formulas to show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Jesus rejected prophecy","Jesus fulfilled Old Testament prophecy","Prophecy is unreliable","The Old Testament is obsolete"]', 'Jesus fulfilled Old Testament prophecy', 8),
(750015, 'Zechariah 9:9 prophesied the Messiah would enter Jerusalem:', 'multiple_choice', '["On a war horse","Riding on a donkey","In a chariot","On foot"]', 'Riding on a donkey', 9),
(750015, 'The unity of Scripture is demonstrated through:', 'multiple_choice', '["Contradictions between testaments","Messianic prophecy and fulfillment","Random coincidences","Human authorship alone"]', 'Messianic prophecy and fulfillment', 10),
(750015, 'Discuss how Messianic prophecy demonstrates the divine inspiration and unity of Scripture.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Messianic prophecies written centuries before Christ''s birth were fulfilled in specific details, demonstrating divine authorship and the unified plan of redemption across both testaments.', 11),
(750016, 'The "Day of the Lord" in prophetic literature refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Any ordinary day","God''s decisive intervention in history for judgment and salvation","A weekly sabbath","A Jewish festival"]', 'God''s decisive intervention in history for judgment and salvation', 1),
(750016, 'Premillennialism teaches that Christ will return:', 'multiple_choice', '["After the millennium","Before the millennium to establish His kingdom","There is no millennium","The millennium is purely spiritual"]', 'Before the millennium to establish His kingdom', 2),
(750016, 'Amillennialism interprets the millennium as:', 'multiple_choice', '["A literal 1000-year reign on earth","The present reign of Christ in heaven and the church","A future golden age","Already past"]', 'The present reign of Christ in heaven and the church', 3),
(750016, 'According to 1 Thessalonians 4:16-17, at Christ''s return believers will:', 'multiple_choice', '["Remain on earth","Be caught up to meet the Lord","Sleep forever","Become angels"]', 'Be caught up to meet the Lord', 4),
(750016, 'The new heavens and new earth represent:', 'multiple_choice', '["Destruction of creation","Renewal and restoration of creation","A purely spiritual existence","Annihilation"]', 'Renewal and restoration of creation', 5),
(750016, 'Daniel 12:2 teaches about:', 'multiple_choice', '["Reincarnation","Bodily resurrection","Soul sleep forever","Annihilation"]', 'Bodily resurrection', 6),
(750016, 'The book of Revelation uses apocalyptic imagery to convey:', 'multiple_choice', '["Literal newspaper predictions","Theological truths about God''s victory","Scientific data","Political commentary only"]', 'Theological truths about God''s victory', 7),
(750016, 'Christians should approach eschatological differences with:', 'multiple_choice', '["Division and hostility","Humility and charity","Indifference","Absolute certainty on all details"]', 'Humility and charity', 8),
(750016, 'The blessed hope of believers is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Earthly prosperity","The return of Christ","Political victory","Personal fame"]', 'The return of Christ', 9),
(750016, 'Eschatological prophecy serves to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Satisfy curiosity about dates","Encourage faithful living and hope","Create fear","Divide the church"]', 'Encourage faithful living and hope', 10),
(750016, 'Explain the purpose of eschatological prophecy and how it should affect the way believers live today.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Eschatological prophecy assures believers of God''s ultimate victory and Christ''s return, motivating holy living, faithful service, and confident hope in the midst of present trials.', 11);
